enrichment.short_description: Bettermode is an all-in-one branded customer community platform for engagement, support, and knowledge sharing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Bettermode is a customer community platform with Ask AI search and drag-and-drop customization, starting at $399/month with a 14-day free trial.

features.title: Drag-and-drop customization
features.description: Build and customize branded community spaces without developers using drag-and-drop tools.

features.title: Ask AI search
features.description: AI-powered federated search helps members find answers across community content (Growth plan and above).

features.title: Granular user roles and permissions
features.description: Control member access and moderation with configurable roles and permission levels.

features.title: Multiple content spaces
features.description: Includes Q&A, Discussion, Events, Polls, Wishlist, Changelog, Roadmap, Knowledge base, Academy, and Help center sections.

features.title: Enterprise integrations
features.description: Connects with Salesforce, HubSpot, Intercom, Segment, Notion, and Slack.

features.title: API and webhooks
features.description: Custom workflows via API, webhooks, and a sandbox environment (Growth plan and above).

features.title: SOC 2 Type II compliance
features.description: Security features including audit logs, SAML, and SOC II compliance on the Premium plan.

pros: No-code drag-and-drop customization for branded communities
Ask AI-powered search included from the Growth tier up
Pre-built integrations with Salesforce, HubSpot, Intercom, Segment, Notion, and Slack
SOC 2 Type II compliance and audit logs available on Premium
Used by notable companies including IBM, HubSpot, Lenovo, and Webflow
cons: Starting price of $399/month is high relative to many community platforms
No permanent free plan, only a 14-day free trial
Ask AI, API/webhooks, and branding removal require the Growth plan or higher
Premium tier pricing is custom/quote-only, requiring a sales conversation

faqs.question: Does Bettermode offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, both the Starter and Growth plans include a 14-day free trial.

faqs.question: How much does Bettermode cost?
faqs.answer: The Starter plan starts at $399/month, the Growth plan starts at $1,500/month, and Premium is custom-priced.

faqs.question: Does Bettermode require developers to set up?
faqs.answer: No, Bettermode is marketed as requiring no developers, with drag-and-drop customization tools.

faqs.question: What integrations does Bettermode support?
faqs.answer: Bettermode integrates with Salesforce, HubSpot, Intercom, Segment, Notion, and Slack, plus API and webhook support for custom workflows.

faqs.question: Is Bettermode SOC 2 compliant?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bettermode states it is SOC 2 Type II compliant and supports GDPR.

use_cases.title: Branded SaaS customer communities
use_cases.description: Launch a branded community for product discussions, support, and engagement.

use_cases.title: Product feedback and roadmap
use_cases.description: Collect feedback and share roadmaps and changelogs with customers via dedicated spaces.

use_cases.title: Self-service knowledge base and help center
use_cases.description: Organize help center and academy content alongside community discussions.

features: Audit logs, SOC II compliance, SAML

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Bettermode?
paragraphs: Bettermode is a branded customer community platform built for engagement, support, and growth. It lets companies host discussions, Q&A, events, polls, a knowledge base, and a product roadmap in one customizable space.
The platform emphasizes no-code, drag-and-drop customization so teams can launch and brand a community without developer involvement, and includes Ask AI-powered search on its higher tiers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Bettermode is for
paragraphs: Bettermode is aimed at SaaS and B2B companies that want a branded community for customer support, engagement, and feedback, with enterprise features like SOC 2 Type II compliance and SAML on its Premium plan for larger organizations.

enrichment.short_description: Circle is an all-in-one platform for building branded communities, online courses, and events with native mobile apps.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Circle combines communities, courses, and events with AI tools and native iOS/Android apps, starting at $89/month with a 14-day free trial.

features.title: Community discussions and feed
features.description: Discussion forums with posts, comments, rich media, a personalized feed, messaging, and a searchable member directory.

features.title: Courses and events
features.description: Host courses and live events, including live streaming and live rooms, alongside community spaces.

features.title: Native iOS and Android apps
features.description: Branded mobile apps with push notifications on iOS and Android.

features.title: Circle AI and AI agents
features.description: AI-powered features including a content co-pilot and, on the Circle Plus plan, AI agents and AI workflows.

features.title: Website builder
features.description: Drag-and-drop website and landing page builder for the community.

features.title: Payments and memberships
features.description: Branded checkout for one-time purchases, memberships, and subscriptions, with tiered transaction fees.

features.title: Workflows and automation
features.description: Automated workflows and custom profile fields available on the Business plan and above.

features.title: APIs and headless access
features.description: Headless Member API, Admin API, and Circle MCP available on Business and higher plans.

pros: Combines community, courses, events, and payments in one product
Native branded iOS and Android apps rather than web wrappers
14-day free trial with no credit card required
30-day, 100% money-back guarantee for paying customers
Used by high-profile creators such as Tim Ferriss and Mel Robbins
cons: No permanent free plan; entry price starts at $89/month
API access, workflows, and branding removal require the Business plan or higher
AI Agents and custom SSO are reserved for the top Circle Plus tier
Circle Plus pricing is custom/quote-only

faqs.question: Does Circle offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, all plans include a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does Circle cost?
faqs.answer: The Professional plan starts at $89/month, Business is $199/month, and Circle Plus is custom-priced.

faqs.question: Does Circle have native mobile apps?
faqs.answer: Yes, Circle offers branded iOS and Android mobile apps with push notifications.

faqs.question: Does Circle refund payments?
faqs.answer: Circle offers a 30-day, 100% money-back guarantee for paying customers.

faqs.question: Can I remove Circle branding?
faqs.answer: Yes, Circle branding removal is included starting with the Business plan.

use_cases.title: Creator communities
use_cases.description: Host a branded community with discussions, events, and paid memberships for an audience.

use_cases.title: Course-based membership businesses
use_cases.description: Sell and deliver courses alongside community discussion and live events.

use_cases.title: Business communities with automation
use_cases.description: Use workflows, custom profile fields, and headless APIs to run more complex community operations.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Circle?
paragraphs: Circle is an all-in-one platform for building a branded community that combines discussions, courses, live events, a website builder, and payment processing.
It offers native branded iOS and Android mobile apps, AI-assisted tools such as a content co-pilot, and, on its top plan, AI agents and workflows for automating community operations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Circle is for
paragraphs: Circle is positioned for creators, coaches, and businesses that want to run community, course, and event experiences under their own brand, with plans that scale from a $89/month Professional tier up to custom enterprise pricing with dedicated support.

enrichment.short_description: Mighty Networks is a community platform for courses, events, and memberships with native iOS and Android apps and an AI Cohost.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Mighty Networks hosts communities, courses, and events with native mobile apps and an AI Cohost, starting at $79/month with a 14-day free trial.

features.title: Native iOS and Android apps
features.description: Real native mobile apps described by Mighty Networks as "not web wrappers," placed on members' home screens.

features.title: AI Cohost
features.description: AI assistance tuned to a community's goals to help run and grow the community.

features.title: Community Design framework
features.description: A proprietary planning framework covering ideal member definition, monthly themes, weekly events, and daily questions.

features.title: Courses and events
features.description: Integrated course delivery, challenges, mini-courses, workshops, and live streaming events.

features.title: Gamification
features.description: Badges, tags, and gamification tools to drive member engagement.

features.title: Payment processing
features.description: Built-in payment processing for memberships, with transaction fees that decrease at higher plan tiers.

features.title: Automations
features.description: Basic automations on the Launch plan, with intermediate automations and API/MCP access on Scale.

pros: True native iOS and Android apps rather than web wrappers
AI Cohost tuned to community goals included
Interface available in seven languages including English, Spanish, German, and French
14-day free trial with no credit card required
Unlimited members and spaces included even on the entry-level Launch plan
cons: No permanent free plan, only a 14-day trial
API/MCP access requires the Scale plan or higher
Transaction fees apply on payments (2% on Launch, down to 0.5% on Mighty Pro)
Scale plan's annual pricing is not published on the pricing page
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not published on the site

faqs.question: Does Mighty Networks offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial is available with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does Mighty Networks cost?
faqs.answer: The Launch plan starts at $79/month ($950/year billed annually), the Scale plan is $179/month, and Mighty Pro is custom-priced.

faqs.question: Does Mighty Networks have native mobile apps?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers native iOS and Android apps rather than web-wrapper apps.

faqs.question: What languages does Mighty Networks support?
faqs.answer: The platform lists support for English, Español, Deutsch, Français, Italiano, Nederlands, and Português.

faqs.question: Does Mighty Networks charge transaction fees?
faqs.answer: Yes, transaction fees range from 2% on the Launch plan down to 0.5% on Mighty Pro.

use_cases.title: Course creator communities
use_cases.description: Sell and deliver courses combined with community engagement and events.

use_cases.title: Coaching and personal development communities
use_cases.description: Run structured member programs using the Community Design framework, challenges, and weekly events.

use_cases.title: Membership businesses at scale
use_cases.description: Use API/MCP access and automations to manage larger paid membership communities.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Mighty Networks?
paragraphs: Mighty Networks is a community platform for hosting communities, courses, events, and paid memberships, built around its own "Community Design" planning framework.
It provides native iOS and Android apps, an AI Cohost for community management, and multilingual support across seven languages.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Mighty Networks is for
paragraphs: Mighty Networks targets course creators, coaches, and personal development businesses building paid membership communities, with plans ranging from a $79/month Launch tier to a custom Mighty Pro tier with branded apps and dedicated strategy support.

enrichment.short_description: Discourse is an open-source community and discussion platform powering more than 22,000 communities, free to self-host or paid from $100/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Discourse is an open-source community platform, free to self-host, with a limited free hosted plan and paid plans starting at $100/month.

features.title: Discussion topics and real-time chat
features.description: Threaded discussion topics alongside real-time chat and private messaging.

features.title: AI-assisted moderation
features.description: AI-assisted community management features, plus a separate Discourse AI product with AI daily credits included on paid plans.

features.title: Theme and plugin customization
features.description: Custom themes and a plugin ecosystem, with 15+ plugins on Pro and 50+ on Enterprise.

features.title: SSO and social logins
features.description: Single sign-on, social logins, and SAML support (Business and Enterprise).

features.title: Searchable knowledge base
features.description: Searchable public knowledge base and gamification features for community engagement.

features.title: GitHub integration
features.description: Connects with GitHub to mirror releases, issues, and pull requests.

features.title: Open-source, self-hostable codebase
features.description: The entire Discourse codebase is freely available on GitHub for self-hosting.

features.title: API and webhooks
features.description: API and webhook access included from the Pro plan and up.

pros: Fully open source with a free self-hosting option
States it has never sold user data and never will
Free hosted plan available to explore core functionality
Extensive plugin ecosystem (15+ on Pro, 50+ on Enterprise)
Used by major organizations including Asana, OpenAI, Zoom, and GitLab
cons: Free hosted plan is limited (2 staff seats, 10 categories, 5GB storage)
Self-hosting requires technical setup and server management
Enterprise plan pricing is custom/quote-only
No published details on founding year, headquarters, or company size

faqs.question: Is Discourse free?
faqs.answer: Discourse is open source and free to self-host, and also offers a free hosted plan with limits (2 staff seats, 10 categories, 5GB storage).

faqs.question: How much does hosted Discourse cost?
faqs.answer: Paid hosted plans start at Pro for $100/month, Business for $500/month, with Enterprise at custom pricing.

faqs.question: Does Discourse offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pro and Business plans offer a free trial with no credit card required up front.

faqs.question: Does Discourse have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Discourse Hub mobile app is available on iOS and Android.

faqs.question: Does Discourse sell user data?
faqs.answer: No, Discourse states it has never sold user data and never will.

use_cases.title: Developer and open-source communities
use_cases.description: Host technical discussion forums with GitHub integration for releases, issues, and pull requests.

use_cases.title: Product feedback and support hubs
use_cases.description: Run support and feedback forums with searchable knowledge base content.

use_cases.title: Team knowledge bases
use_cases.description: Use categories, tags, and search to organize internal or public team knowledge.

features: Limited functionality for exploring the platform

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Discourse?
paragraphs: Discourse is a customizable, scalable community platform powering more than 22,000 communities. Its full codebase is open source and available on GitHub, and it can be self-hosted for free or run on Discourse's managed hosting.

The platform offers discussion topics, real-time chat, AI-assisted moderation features, a searchable knowledge base, and an extensive theme and plugin ecosystem, with paid hosted plans starting at $100/month for Pro.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Discourse is for
paragraphs: Discourse is used by developer and open-source communities, product support and feedback teams, and organizations such as Asana, OpenAI, Zoom, and GitLab that need a self-hostable, data-owning discussion platform with a free entry point.

name: Vanilla Forums
skip_reason: vanillaforums.com now performs a 301 redirect through vanilla.higherlogic.com to https://www.higherlogic.com/vanilla/. The product operates exclusively under the 'Higher Logic Vanilla' brand with no independent Vanilla Forums site or identity remaining. Enriching this entry would duplicate the separate higher-logic-vanilla listing.

enrichment.short_description: Hivebrite is a community engagement platform for building branded member communities with events, forums, and AI-powered matching.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Hivebrite is an online community platform with AI matching, events, forums, and mobile apps for associations, alumni networks, and nonprofits.

features.title: AI-Powered Matching
features.description: Uses AI to spark 1:1 connections between community members at scale, alongside AI-driven content and people recommendations.

features.title: No-Code Page Builder
features.description: A drag-and-drop builder lets organizations design and brand their community pages without coding.

features.title: Member Directory
features.description: Advanced search and filtering tools help members find and connect with each other.

features.title: Events
features.description: Hosts virtual and in-person events with RSVP and check-in functionality.

features.title: Forums
features.description: Provides discussion spaces for peer learning and community conversation.

features.title: Multi-language & Live Translation
features.description: Supports multiple languages with live translation to help communities reach a global audience.

features.title: Native Mobile Apps
features.description: Branded native iOS and Android apps extend community engagement to mobile devices.

features.title: Community Analytics
features.description: Provides performance metrics and engagement insights through a back-office analytics dashboard.

pros: Native iOS and Android mobile apps available
AI-powered matching and content/people recommendations for members
No-code drag-and-drop page builder for branding the community
Multi-language support with live translation for global communities
ISO 27001 certified and compliant with GDPR, CCPA, PCI-DSS, and TX-RAMP
Used by over 2,000 organizations worldwide
cons: No free trial or free plan is advertised; the site directs prospects to request a demo
Entry-level Core plan starts at $895/month billed annually, a high entry cost for small organizations
Higher-tier Prime and Custom Fit plans require a custom quote, so full pricing isn't transparent
Mobile app, multi-language support, connectors, and mentoring modules are sold as optional add-ons rather than included in base plans

faqs.question: Does Hivebrite offer a free trial or free plan?
faqs.answer: No free trial or free plan is mentioned on Hivebrite's pricing page; prospective customers are directed to request a demo or talk to sales.

faqs.question: How much does Hivebrite cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing starts at $895/month (billed annually) for the Core plan. The Flex plan starts at $1,995/month, while Prime and Custom Fit plans require a custom quote.

faqs.question: Does Hivebrite have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, Hivebrite offers native iOS and Android mobile apps, listed as part of its Build and Launch feature sets (and as an add-on depending on plan).

faqs.question: Does Hivebrite support multiple languages?
faqs.answer: Yes, Hivebrite offers multi-language support with live translation as a feature, available as an add-on according to the pricing page.

faqs.question: Is Hivebrite secure and compliant?
faqs.answer: Hivebrite states it is ISO 27001 certified and compliant with GDPR, CCPA, PCI-DSS, and TX-RAMP, with 24/7 threat monitoring.

use_cases.title: Association member communities
use_cases.description: Associations use Hivebrite's directory, forums, events, and membership tools to engage members and manage renewals.

use_cases.title: Alumni network engagement
use_cases.description: Higher education institutions use Hivebrite's mentoring, directory, and mobile app features to keep alumni connected and engaged.

use_cases.title: Nonprofit and mission-driven communities
use_cases.description: Nonprofits use Hivebrite's groups, donations, and communications tools to build and sustain supporter communities.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Hivebrite?
paragraphs: Hivebrite is a community engagement platform used by organizations to build branded online communities that combine directories, forums, events, messaging, and membership management in one place.

The platform includes AI-powered matching and recommendation tools designed to connect members with relevant people and content, alongside a no-code page builder for branding and native iOS/Android mobile apps for on-the-go engagement.
Hivebrite states it is used by over 2,000 organizations and holds ISO 27001 certification along with GDPR, CCPA, PCI-DSS, and TX-RAMP compliance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Hivebrite is for
paragraphs: Hivebrite is built for associations, higher education institutions managing alumni networks, and nonprofit organizations that need to run member directories, events, forums, and mentoring programs within a single branded community platform. Pricing starting at $895/month makes it best suited to organizations with a formal community program budget rather than very small or informal groups.

name: Mobilize
skip_reason: www.mobilize.io performs a 301 redirect to https://www.forj.ai/forj-community, whose page title reads 'Mobilize is now Forj Community' and headline states 'Mobilize is now Forj.' The product was rebranded to Forj Connect in 2023 and no longer operates under the independent Mobilize name (only legacy app.mobilize.io / support.mobilize.io login links remain).

enrichment.short_description: B2B/B2C customer community platform, originally Vanilla Forums (2009), acquired by Higher Logic in 2021.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Higher Logic Vanilla is a B2B/B2C community platform (formerly Vanilla Forums) offering forums, gamification, and AI-powered community insights.

features.title: Self-service & Q&A support
features.description: Forums and Q&A tools let members ask questions and share solutions, helping deflect support cases.

features.title: Drag-and-drop layout editor
features.description: Customize community page layouts without needing developer resources.

features.title: Gamification
features.description: Badges, points, and leaderboards to drive member engagement.

features.title: Federated search
features.description: Search across the community and other connected content sources from one place.

features.title: Polling and product feedback
features.description: Collect member input and feature requests directly within the community.

features.title: Community analytics
features.description: Pre-built and custom reports to track community performance and health.

features.title: AI-powered insights
features.description: Monitors sentiment and tracks community health using AI.

features.title: AI Bot Shield
features.description: Detects and blocks bot activity within the community.

pros: AI-powered sentiment and community health monitoring built in
Dedicated AI Bot Shield for bot detection
Gamification (badges, points, leaderboards) included
Drag-and-drop customization without developer resources
Integrates with ChatGPT, Claude, and Cursor via MCP
Established product lineage dating back to Vanilla Forums, founded 2009
cons: No public pricing; three tiers (Corporate, Enterprise, Enterprise Plus) require booking a demo with sales
No free trial or free plan mentioned on the site
Company size and supported languages not disclosed publicly

faqs.question: Is Higher Logic Vanilla the same as Vanilla Forums?
faqs.answer: Yes. Vanilla Forums was founded in 2009 in Montreal, Quebec, Canada, and was acquired by Higher Logic in 2021, which rebranded it as Higher Logic Vanilla.

faqs.question: Does Higher Logic Vanilla offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: The pages reviewed do not mention a free trial or free plan; pricing tiers require booking a demo with sales.

faqs.question: What pricing plans does Higher Logic Vanilla offer?
faqs.answer: Three tiers are listed — Corporate, Enterprise, and Enterprise Plus — differentiated by feature scale, but exact prices are not published and require contacting sales.

faqs.question: Does Higher Logic Vanilla include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers AI-powered insights for sentiment and community health monitoring, an AI Bot Shield for bot detection, and integrations with ChatGPT, Claude, and Cursor via MCP.

use_cases.title: Customer self-service communities
use_cases.description: Companies use Higher Logic Vanilla to let customers ask questions, share solutions, and deflect support cases through forums and Q&A.
use_cases.audience: B2B/B2C support and customer success teams

use_cases.title: Association and member communities
use_cases.description: Professional and trade associations use the platform to host member discussions, gather feedback via polling, and manage engagement.

features: Additional tools to support more advanced community structures and multi-product companies
Advanced capabilities that scale with large, advanced community use cases
Support for the most advanced communities and the most ambitious community use cases

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Higher Logic Vanilla?
paragraphs: Higher Logic Vanilla is a community-first B2B/B2C customer community platform. It originated as Vanilla Forums, founded in 2009 in Montreal, Quebec, Canada, and was acquired by Higher Logic in 2021, which rebranded it as Higher Logic Vanilla.
The platform provides forums, Q&A, gamification, federated search, polling, and community analytics, alongside AI-powered sentiment monitoring and bot detection (AI Bot Shield).

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Higher Logic Vanilla is for
paragraphs: It targets B2B and B2C companies, professional and trade associations, and user groups looking to build self-service support and engagement communities, from mid-market to enterprise scale.

enrichment.short_description: Khoros Communities (now Khoros Aurora AI) is an enterprise platform for building customer forums, knowledge bases, and idea communities.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Khoros Aurora AI is an enterprise community platform with forums, knowledge base, ideas, and AI-powered moderation and answers.

features.title: Forums & Q&A
features.description: Core discussion forums and Q&A for peer-to-peer support.

features.title: Knowledge base
features.description: Self-service knowledge content to help deflect support cases.

features.title: Ideas & product feedback
features.description: Collect and prioritize member ideas and feedback.

features.title: Events and groups
features.description: Organize community events and interest- or region-based groups.

features.title: Human Engine
features.description: Gamification, reputation, badges, leaderboards, and expert recognition to drive engagement.

features.title: Intelligence Layer
features.description: Includes a Community Language Model (CLM), Answer Assist (Beta), AI Moderation (Beta), and Orchestrator (Beta).

features.title: Action Layer
features.description: Rules-based automation with optional LLM integration for triggered actions.

pros: Long operating history, with 4,000+ communities powered since 2001
Reports significant self-service cost savings ($500M+) and 2-5x revenue lift from engaged members across its customer base
Built-in AI features: Answer Assist, AI Moderation, and Orchestrator
Supports multiple languages: English, Spanish, Italian, French, German, and Czech
Free migration offer from Community Classic to Aurora AI through the end of 2026 for existing customers
cons: Key AI features (Answer Assist, AI Moderation, Orchestrator) are still in Beta
No public pricing published; requires contacting sales
No free trial or free plan mentioned
Recent rebrand from 'Community Classic' to 'Aurora AI' may create a naming transition for existing users

faqs.question: What is Khoros Aurora AI?
faqs.answer: It is Khoros's community platform, built with React and GraphQL, combining forums, knowledge base, ideas, events, and groups with AI-driven engagement tools.

faqs.question: Does Khoros offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: The pages reviewed do not disclose a free trial or free plan; pricing is not publicly listed.

faqs.question: What AI features does Khoros Communities include?
faqs.answer: The Intelligence Layer includes a Community Language Model (CLM), Answer Assist, AI Moderation, and Orchestrator — all currently in Beta, with more capabilities planned through Q1 2027.

faqs.question: Is there a migration path from older Khoros community products?
faqs.answer: Yes, Khoros offers free migration from Community Classic to Aurora AI through the end of 2026 for existing Classic clients.

use_cases.title: Enterprise customer support communities
use_cases.description: Organizations use Khoros to reduce support costs through self-service forums and knowledge bases.
use_cases.audience: Enterprise customer support and community teams

use_cases.title: Industry-specific engagement communities
use_cases.description: Khoros markets tailored solutions for Financial Services, Retail, Technology, Telecommunications, and Travel/Hospitality sectors.
use_cases.audience: Financial services, retail, technology, telecom, and travel/hospitality brands

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Khoros Communities?
paragraphs: Khoros Communities, now marketed as Khoros Aurora AI, is an enterprise community platform built with React and GraphQL. Khoros, based in Austin, TX, states it has powered 4,000+ communities since 2001.

The platform combines forums, knowledge base, ideas, events, and groups with three layers: a Human Engine for gamification and reputation, an Intelligence Layer with AI features like Answer Assist and AI Moderation, and an Action Layer for rules-based automation.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Khoros Communities is for
paragraphs: Khoros markets its community platform to enterprises in Financial Services, Retail, Technology, Telecommunications, and Travel/Hospitality looking to reduce support costs and grow revenue through engaged member communities.

name: inSided
skip_reason: insided.com now redirects (via tools.gainsight.com/insided/) to gainsight.com/customer-communities, which presents the product exclusively as 'Gainsight Customer Communities' with no mention of the 'inSided' name anywhere on the page. The product has been fully rebranded following its acquisition by Gainsight, so it no longer operates under the 'inSided' name as required to enrich under that name.
